lead_headline: Foreclosure filings surge 145% week-over-week to 49 new cases worth $22M, while restaurant churn slows to 18 openings against 16 closures after last week's brutal 47-closure bloodbath.

restaurant.blurb: 18 openings, 16 closures over the past seven days — a dramatic cooling from the prior week's 28-open-47-closed carnage. The market's finding its feet again, with notable action in Platt Park (Reunion Bread bakery) and Union Station (Takiza Taco shuttered). Even Quiznos can't decide if it's coming or going in Thornton.

foreclosure.blurb: 49 new filings this week — up 145% from last week's 20 — totaling $22.2M in distressed property value. The headliner: a $8.1M commercial filing on Clinton Street in Arapahoe County. Weld and Larimer lead volume at 285 and 259 active cases respectively, while 50 auctions loom in the next week.

retail.blurb: Retail tracker in development — comprehensive storefront monitoring across Metro Denver coming soon.

revenue.blurb: Revenue intelligence tool planned — sales tax data and business performance metrics on deck.

reputation.blurb: Reputation monitoring system planned — tracking review trends and consumer sentiment shifts.
